observations: ct-item-list renders no images/icons of its own; any non-text content lives inside the slotted ct-item-list-item children and is that content's own responsibility.

standard: 1.2.3 Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded) (Level A)

observations: Wrapping every ct-item-list-item child in a real <li class="ct-item-list__item"> inside a <ul class="ct-item-list"> gives assistive technology accurate list/listitem role and item-count information regardless of what content each item wraps. Establishing that structure for whatever is slotted in is this component's entire purpose, so the criterion is met unconditionally rather than depending on the implementer to add it separately.

The flex-based horizontal/vertical layouts never use the CSS `order` property, so the visual/reading order of items always matches the DOM order of the ct-item-list-item children the implementer supplies.
The `direction` prop controls layout axis for design purposes, not in response to device orientation, and content is not restricted to a single display orientation.
ct-item-list renders no form inputs.
ct-item-list applies no color of its own (its upstream CivicTheme SCSS is gap/layout only) — any color comes from slotted content and is that content's responsibility.
ct-item-list renders no text or foreground/background color of its own; contrast is entirely a property of whatever is slotted into each item.

The horizontal layout's flex-wrap: wrap lets items reflow onto additional lines as their content grows when text is resized to 200%, rather than clipping or requiring horizontal scrolling; the vertical layout is already single-column and simply grows taller.
ct-item-list renders no text of its own.

flex-wrap: wrap on the horizontal direction means items wrap onto new lines instead of requiring two-dimensional scrolling at a 320px-equivalent viewport; the vertical direction is inherently single-column and never needs horizontal scrolling.
ct-item-list draws no borders, focus indicators, or other graphical UI components of its own (border/margin/padding on the root are reset to 0).
ct-item-list sets no line-height, letter-spacing, or word-spacing of its own, and imposes no fixed-height/overflow:hidden container that would clip re-spaced text in slotted content.

standard: 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus (Level AA)

observations: ct-item-list itself introduces no interactive/focusable elements; any keyboard behavior belongs to whatever is slotted into each ct-item-list-item.

standard: 2.3.1 Three Flashes or Below Threshold (Level A)

observations: As with 1.3.2, the layout never reassigns visual order via the CSS `order` property, so any focusable elements inside slotted items receive focus in an order that matches both the DOM and the visual layout.
ct-item-list renders no links of its own; link purpose is the responsibility of whatever is slotted into each item (e.g. a ct-link or ct-content-link).
ct-item-list renders no headings or labels of its own.
ct-item-list defines no focus styling of its own; any focus indicator belongs to whatever interactive element is slotted into an item.
ct-item-list renders no accessible-name-bearing interactive controls of its own.
ct-item-list defines no pointer targets of its own; target size is the responsibility of whatever interactive element is slotted into an item.
Compliance depends on the implementer setting the correct lang attribute on the html element, not on this component.
Compliance depends on the implementer setting a lang attribute on an item's content when that item's language differs from the page's.
ct-item-list is not itself focusable and triggers no context change on focus.
ct-item-list has no input props that trigger behavior — `direction`/`size`/`no-gap` only affect layout, never navigation or context.
Compliance depends on the implementer using ct-item-list consistently (same direction/size) for the same kind of list wherever it recurs across the site.
ct-item-list is a layout primitive, not a form control; it provides no labels or instructions of its own.

The native <ul>/<li> elements this component renders automatically expose the "list" role and per-item "listitem" role (with correct position-in-set/set-size) to assistive technology without any custom ARIA needed. Name/Role/Value for interactive content within an item is that content's own responsibility.
ct-item-list has no dynamic/live-updating behavior — it renders once from its children with no state changes of its own to announce.
